261.reason: [The ERROR token pattern "[&|]" is intended to match single '&' or '|' to indicate error type 'a', but the Lexer's addToken method may not reliably generate this token because the matching loop depends on the enum order and regex matching. However, the more critical issue is that if any character sequence does not match any token pattern (including ERROR), the Lexer prints an error but does not add an ERROR token to the tokens list, causing the Compiler to miss the error. In the specific case of single '&' or '|', the ERROR pattern should match, but if for any reason it doesn't (e.g., due to regex engine behavior or input encoding), no error token is produced. The design should ensure that any unrecognized lexeme results in an explicit ERROR token being added.]

205.code: System.err.println("No valid token found at line " + currentLine + " at index " + currentIndex);
205.reason: [This line indicates that no token pattern matched, but the method does not create an ERROR token or advance the currentIndex, leading to potential infinite loops and missed error reporting. For error type 'a' (single '&' or '|'), the ERROR pattern should have matched in the loop above, but if it fails to do so for any reason, this fallback does not handle it properly by generating an ERROR token. The Lexer must ensure that every invalid lexeme results in an ERROR token being added to the tokens list so that Compiler.analyze() can detect it.]
